events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a structural welding inspection where the field photo is paired with a cropped sheet excerpt, but the title block, revision, and matching callout are not visible. This means the installed condition cannot be tied to the controlling page. Reference material is AWS D1.1.

The key issue here is that the work cannot be verified against the controlling document because the drawing identification (title block, revision, callout) is not visible. Without being able to tie the installed condition to the controlling page, we cannot confirm compliance.

This is a "needs_more_info" situation - we can't verify the work against the controlling document because the document identification is missing from the excerpt.

Let me construct the JSON response.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "needs_more_info",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.7,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drawing title block, revision, and matching callout not visible in cropped excerpt — installed weld condition cannot be tied to the controlling page",
    "Unable to verify weld size, length, profile, or location against engineered requirements without the controlling drawing identification",
    "AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria cannot be applied without confirming the applicable weld symbol and specification requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Obtain the full drawing sheet with visible title block, revision number, and weld callouts for the observed connection",
    "Confirm drawing revision is current and approved for construction before accepting the weld",
    "Re-inspect the weld against the controlling drawing and AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria once document traceability is established"
  ],
  "rationale": "The weld has reached inspection stage, but the controlling document cannot be identified from the cropped excerpt. Without title block, revision, and callout visibility, there is no traceability between the installed condition and the engineered requirements. AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria depend on knowing the specified weld parameters, so the work cannot be definitively passed or failed until the controlling drawing is provided.",
  "references": [
    "AWS D1.1 — Clause 6 (Inspection)",
    "AWS D1.1 — Clause 5 (Fabrication)",
    "AWS D1.1 — Clause 7 (Stud Welding) if applicable"
  ]
}
